def: (part. act. V. of الم) adj. Grieved, sad, afflicted, pained, discontented.

ety: act. part. of تأمّل 'to consider carefully,' &c., v of أمل 'to hope'

subs: adv. After reflexion, or due consideration.

def: (part. act. V. of امل) adj. Contemplative, considering, reflecting, reflector, meditating, meditative, thoughtful, examining attentively.

ety: pass. part. of تعارف 'to be acquainted with one another,' vi of عرف 'to know'
def: part. adj. Known to each other, mutually known.
more: part. pass. (VI. of عرف) Known, mutually known.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian متعارف (muta'ārif), from Arabic مُتَعَارِف (mutaʕārif).

ety: act. part. of تعرّف 'to acquaint oneself with,' v of عرف 'to know'
def: adj. Making oneself acquainted with; — curious to know, prying into the secrets (of another); — making confession; — s.m. One who is curious to know, &c.; — one who confesses.

ety: act. part. of تعرّض, v of (and syn. with
def: adj. Opposed (to), averse (to); — s.m. Opposer, hinderer, withstander; one who causes pain or trouble.
more: (part. act. V. from عرض) s. m. Opposing, resisting, giving pain or trouble.

ety: act. part. of تعصّب 'to support, defend; to be zealous (for
def: adj. Attached to one's people, helping them; — partial, prejudiced; zealous; bigoted; superstitious.
more: (part. act. V. of عصب) adj. Partial, bigoted, superstitious, prejudiced.

ety: act. part. of تأسّف, v of أسف 'to grieve exceedingly'
def: part. adj. Grieving, oppressed with grief, groaning, sorrowful, repentant; anxious.
(part. act. of V. of اسف) adj. Sorrowful, oppressed with grief, repentant.

ety: act. part. of تاثّر 'to receive an impression,' v. of اثر 'to make a mark' (upon
def: part. adj. Producing effect or impression, efficacious, effective; — impressive, touching, affecting; — affected, touched, impressed.

ety: act. part. of تعسّر, v of (and syn. with
def: adj. Difficult; impossible; intricate, hard to be explained.
more: (part. act. V. of عسر) adj. Difficult, intricate.

more: (part. act. V. of اثر) adj. Producing effect or impression, efficacious, affected.

def: (part. act. V. of اذي Heb. Chald. ) adj. Afflicted, troubled, grieved, injured.

ety: act. part. of تأذّي 'to be or become annoyed or molested,' &c. v of rt. أذي; formed from aẕā, 'annoyance,' &c.
def: part. adj. Annoyed, molested, injured, afflicted, vexed, grieved.

ety: act. part. of تعذر 'to urge an excuse for oneself,' &c.; v of عذر 'to excuse,' &c.
def: adj. Making an excuse or an apology; — deserving of pardon, excusable; — difficult, impracticable, impossible; — defaced, effaced (a trace, or vestige); — stained, besmeared (with), defiled; — s.m. One who excuses himself; an apologist.
more: (part. act. V. from عذر) adj. 1. Making an apology. 2. Deserving pardon. 3. Difficult, impossible. 4. Stained. 5. Defaced.

ety: for A. متابعة, inf. n. of تابع tābaʻa, 'to make (one thing
def: s.f. Following; imitating; conforming oneself (to), submitting, obeying; submission, obedience; obsequiousness; — doing (anything) solidly and certainly; — doing (a thing) in the same manner after another
more: (inf. III. of تبع) s. f. 1. Obsequiousness. 2. Following, understanding, or imitating one another, submitting, obeying. 3. Doing any thing solidly and certainly, doing in the same manner after another.

ety: act. part. of تبدّل 'to become changed or altered,' v of بدل 'to change or exchange'
def: part. adj. Changing alternately; alternate; interchangeable; inverting; — changed, altered; — s.m. One who changes, or substitutes, or inverts.
more: (part. act. V. of بدل) adj. Changing alternately, alternate, interchangeable, inverting.

ety: for A. متبادلة mutabādilat, fem. of mutabādil, act. part. of تبادل 'to make an exchange with (another
def: part. adj. Making an exchange with; taking turn about; alternate (syn. mutabaddil).

ety: act. part. of تبحّر 'to enlarge (one's own mind
def: part. adj. Very learned, deeply read or skilled (in), profound.

ety: for A. مطابقة, inf. n. of طابق; see mut̤ābiq
def: s.f. Conformity, congruity, accordance, correspondence; uniformity; proportion; analogy; similarity, sameness, equality.
more: (inf. III. of طبق) s. f. Conformity, equality, identity, analogy, correspondence.

ety: pass. part. of تبنّي 'to adopt' (as a son
def: part. adj. & s.m. Adopted (as a son); — an adopted son (syn. le-pālak)
more: n. m. ابن a son. An adopted child ( le-pālak ). mutabannā k. , To adopt a child ( god lenā , 2).
(part. pass. V. of بني Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Adopted (son).
subs: (act. part. of the same conj.), part. adj. & s.m. Adopting; adoptive (father); — one who adopts as his son
(-ko), To adopt (a child; — syn. god lenā).

ety: abst. s. fr. next, q.v.
def: s.f. Adoption; the state of being an adopted child.

ety: pass. part. of تبرّك 'to have a blessing, to be blessed,' v of برك; but prob. formed fr. barakat, q.v.
def: part. adj. Blessed, holy, sacred, sanctified, consecrated; — revered, venerable; — heavenly, angelic; — fortunate, happy, august.
more: adj. 1. See mubārik 2. Heavenly; angelic. 3. Revered; venerable. Mutabarrak ḍāṛhī .
(part. pass. V. of برک) adj. Fortunate, august, blessed, holy.

ety: for A. متبرکة mutabarrakat, fem. of mutabarrak
def: adj. = mutabarrak, q.v. part. adj. Blessed, holy, sacred, sanctified, consecrated; — revered, venerable; — heavenly, angelic; — fortunate, happy, august.

ety: act. part. of تبسّم 'to smile,' v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Smiling, laughing; risible; — flashing (lightning).
more: (part. act. V. of بسم To smile, Chald. To be sweet or pleasant) adj. Smiling, laughing, risible.

ety: act. part. of تبذّل 'to be careless of one's own honour or reputation,' v of بذل 'to give liberally or unsparingly'
def: part. adj. Despicable, not cared for, despised; — s.m. A doer of his own drudgery.
more: (part. act. V. of بذل) adj. Despicable, not cared for, despised.

ety: pass. part. of طبّق 'to cover; to place (one thing
def: part. adj. Covered; (one thing) placed over (another); — complicated; multiplied: folded; doubled, double.
more: (part. pass. II. of طبق) adj. Placed one thing over another, doubled, folded.

ety: act. part. of طابق 'to suit, match, tally, conform, correspond,' &c., iii of طبق; see t̤abaq
def: v.t. To make similar or like (to); to reconcile; to compare (with)
more: adj. طبق Like ( barābar , 1, 10). mutābiq , adv. In comformity or accordance with (حسب) mutābiq k , v. a. 1. To compare with. 2. To make like; reconcile. mutābiq h. , v. n. To agree; correspond; coincide.
(part. act. III. of طبق) adj. Conformable, suitable, equal, identical, answering, correspondent, agreeable to.
subs: (-ke), To agree, or correspond (with); to coincide (with), &c.

ety: act. part. of تديّن, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Religious, devout; firm in religion; orthodox; upright, honest, just (syn. diyānat-dār).
more: adj. دين obeying. 1. Orthodox; religious. 2. Honest; just ( dyānat-dār ).
(part. act. V. of دين) adj. Orthodox, religious, honest.

ety: pass. part. of تداول 'to take, or have, or do, by turns,' vi of دول 'to come round' (as a day, a turn, &c.
def: part. adj. Passed round from hand to hand; bandied to and fro; taken turn and turn about; — alternate; — common; known; customary, usual.

ety: for A. متداولة mutadāwalat, fem. of mutadāwal
def: adj. = mutadāwal, q.v. part. adj. Passed round from hand to hand; bandied to and fro; taken turn and turn about; — alternate; — common; known; customary, usual.

ety: act. part. of تفحّص, v of (and syn. with
def: s.m. Inquiring, asking, questioning; — an inquisitor, investigator, examiner; — mutafaḥḥaṣ (pass. part. of the same conj.), part. Questioned, examined.
more: part. act. (V. of فحص) Inquiring, asking.

ety: act. part. of تفکّر, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Thinking, considering, reflecting; thoughtful, contemplative, given to consideration; serious, pensive, grave, concerned, anxious, uneasy or disturbed in mind, perplexed, perturbed; amazed
more: adj. فکر thought upon. Thoughtful; contemplative; pensive.
(part. act. V. from فکر) adj. Contemplative, considering, thinking, reflecting, serious, thoughtful, concerned, pensive, grave.
subs: conj. part. Becoming thoughtful, or pensive, &c.; — adv. Pensively, gravely; anxiously, &c.

ety: prob. corr. of A. متفنن mutafannin, act. part. of تفننّ, 'to practise various arts,' &c., v. of فنّ 'to trick, to deceive,' &c.
def: adj. Artful, crafty, vicious.

ety: act. part. of تفرّق 'to be or become separated,' &c., v of فرق 'to make a separation' (between
def: part. adj. Separated, dispersed, scattered; dissipated; confused; ruined; — separate, distinct, different; various; miscellaneous
more: adj. فرق separated. 1. Miscellaneous ( bhāṅt bhāṅt kā ). 2. Scattered; dispersed. mutafarraq k . To separate; scatter; disperse; dissolve.
(part. act. V. from فرق Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Separate, distinct, dispersed, scattered. Plur. متفرقات mutafarriḳāt, Various and scattered things.
subs: v.t. To separate, to divide; to scatter, disperse; to dissolve.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُتَفَرِّق (mutafarriq), borrowed from Arabic مُتَفَرِّق (mutafarriq), from تَفَرَّقَ (tafarraqa).

ety: for A. متفرقة mutafarriqat, fem. of mutafarriq
def: adj. = mutafarriq, q.v.; — s.m. Horse-guards, or spearmen, or archers, attending a king when he travels.
more: adj. fem. of متفرق mutafarriḳ, q. v.

def: s.m. pl. (of mutafarriqat, fem. of mutafarriq), Scattered things; various things; sundries, miscellaneous articles; — the various items (of an account); — separate and scattered portions of land belonging to a village or an estate.
more: n. 1. Sundries; miscellaneous articles. 2. The several items of an account. 3. Separate and scattered portions of land included in a village or an estate.

ety: act. part. of تفطّن 'to understand,' &c., v of فطن 'to become intelligent'
def: adj. Thoughtful, considerate.

ety: act. part. of تفاوت 'to be or become far apart, or widely distinct,' v of فوت 'to pass away, escape,' &c.
def: adj. Wide apart, distinct, separate; distant; different, diverse.
more: (part. act. iii. of فوت) adj. Different, diverse, mutually differing.

ety: act. part. of تغير 'to become altered or changed,' v of غير; but quasi-pass. of غيّر (ii of غير
def: part. adj. Altered, changed (gen. for the worse); — disturbed, perplexed, stupefied; — changing, varying; mutable; inconsistent.
more: (part. act. V. from غير) adj. Changed, inconsistent, disturbed, stupified, perplexed.

ety: act. part. of تغّلب, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Overcoming; victorious; dominant, powerful.
more: (part. act. V. from غلب) adj. Victorious, overcoming.

ety: act. part. of تحيّر, v of (and syn. with

more: adj. حير confounded. Amazed; confounded ( bhuchak ).
(part. act. V. of حير) adj. Astonished, wondering, confounded, amazed.

ety: act. part. of تحقّق 'to be or become proved,' v of حقّ 'to be right or true'
def: part. adj. Certain, true; — s.m. A verifier
more: adj. حق was true. Proved; verified; true ( tahqīq ). mutahaqaq k. , v. a. To verify; prove; establish.
subs: (pass. part. of the same conj.), part. adj. Proved, verified; established; — true; sure, certain

ety: act. part. of تحمّد 'to affect praise,' v of حمد 'to praise'
def: part. adj. Praised; deserving of praise, commendable.
more: (part. act. v. of حمد) adj. Praised, deserving of praise.

(part. act. V. of حمل Heb. ) adj. Affable, considerate, enduring, patient, suffering, bearing a burthen, passive.

more: adj. حرک moved. 1. Moving; moveable. 2. Accented with the short vowels.
(part. act. V. of حرک) adj. 1. Moved, moving oneʼs self, moveable. غير متحرک g̠ẖair-mutaḥarrik, Immovable. 2. Accented with short vowels.
subs: adj. Immoveable; — not accented with short vowels.

ety: for A. متحرکة mutaḥarrikat, fem. of mutaḥarrik
def: adj. = mutaḥarrik, q.v.
subs: Moveable powers. part. adj. Moved; moving oneself; moveable; — accented with short vowels
adj. Immoveable; — not accented with short vowels.

ety: act. part. of تحرّم 'to become in a state of prohibition;' 'to become entitled to reverence, &c.'; v of حرم 'to be or become forbidden'
def: part. adj. Forbidden, prohibited; unlawful; — revered, respected.
more: (part. act. V. of حرم Heb. ) adj. Revered, respected, prohibited, unlawful.

ety: act. part. of تہاون 'to think lightly' (of
def: part. adj. Holding light or cheap, despising; neglecting; negligent; incurious; — s.m. One who despises, &c.

ety: act. part. of طہّر 'to cleanse, purify,' &c., ii of طہر 'to be or become clean, or pure'
def: adj. & s.m. Cleansing, purifying, purificatory; cleanser, purifier
more: (part. act. II. of طہر Heb. and Chald. ) s. Purifier. ¶ mut̤ahhar (part. pass.) adj. Purified, holy.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Cleansed, purified; pure, holy.

def: adj. & s.m. corr. of متعاهد mutaʻāhid, q.v. part. adj. Confederate; covenanted; — s.m. A contractor; one who enters into an engagement for the revenue

(from موٿا) s. f. Fatness, &c.: see موٿاٴي moṭā-ī.

ety: pass. part. of طيّب 'to render sweet, or savoury, &c.; to perfume,' ii of طاب (for طيب
def: part. Perfumed; spiced; aromatized; aromatic.

more: (part. pass. V. of جلو Heb. ) adj. Splendid.

ety: act. part. of تجسّس, v of (and syn. with

more: (part. act. V. of جسّ Heb. ) s. m. A spy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian متجسس (mutajassis), from Arabic مُتَجَسِّس (mutajassis).

ety: act. part. of تکبرّ 'to be proud,' &c., v of کبر 'to be great or large'
def: adj. Proud, haughty, arrogant, disdainful.
more: (part. act. V. from کبر Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Proud, haughty, arrogant, disdainful, fastidious.

ety: mutakabbir, q.v.+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. Haughty, &c. ( = mutakabbir); — adv. Proudly, haughtily, arrogantly.

ety: act. part. of تقدّم 'to advance; to precede,' v of قدم 'to go before, to precede'
def: adj. Preceding, past, anterior, ancient, antique; — s.m. An ancient; an ancestor; — a chief, president, principal, gevernor.
more: (part. act. V. from قدم Heb. ) adj. Ancient, anteriour, antique, preceding; (subst.) A chief, president, guardian. Plur. متقدمين mutaḳaddimīn, The ancients, ancient.

def: (obl. pl. of mutaqaddim), s.m. pl. The ancients.
more: n. m. قدم went before. The ancients; classics.

ety: act. part. of تکفّل, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Becoming surety (for), undertaking; — surety, bail, bondsman.
more: part. act. (V. of کفل) Becoming surety, undertaking; bail or surety (the person).

ety: act. part. of تکلّم 'to speak,' v of کلم
def: part. adj. & s.m. Speaking; — a speaker; orator; declaimer; — (in Gram.) the first person.
more: n. m. کلم 1. A speaker; declaimer. 2. ( Gram .) The first person.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic مُتَكَلِّم (mutakallim).

def: (part. act. V. from کلام) adj. 1. Speaking; (subst.) A speaker, declaimer. 2. The first person (in grammar). (Plur. متکلمين mutakallimīn ).

ety: act. part. of تقّرب, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Drawing near, approaching (to); having access (to); — adjacent, near; allied (to); — s.m. One who has free access to a king; a royal relation, or favourite.
more: (part. act. V. from قرب Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Approaching, having access; (subst.) A royal relation, or favourite.

ety: act. part. of تقّرر, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Fixed, settled, confirmed, established.
more: part. act. (V. of قرّ) Fixed, established. (Fem. متقرره).

ety: for A. متقررة mutaqarrirat, fem. of mutaqarrir
def: adj. = mutaqarrir, q.v. part. adj. Fixed, settled, confirmed, established.

ety: act. part. of تقاطع 'to cut each other,' &c., vi of قطع 'to cut,'
def: part. adj. Cutting, intersecting (two or more lines); — parted from each other, detached, separate
subs: v.n. To cut or intersect one another (two or more lines).

ety: act. part. of تقاضی 'to exact; to dun,' &c., vi of قضي 'to allot, determine, decree,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Exacting, or receiving (payment of a debt); importunate; — one who exacts payment.
more: (part. act. VI. from قضي Heb. ) adj. Exacting or receiving (payment of a debt); an exactor, a dun.

ety: act. part. of تحيّل 'to image (a thing

more: (part. act. V. of خيل Heb. ) adj. Imagining, suspecting, fancying (good or evil).

ety: for A. متخيلة mutaḵẖaiyilat, fem. of mutaḵẖaiyil
def: part. adj. = mutaḵẖaiyil; — s.m. Imagination, fancy
more: n. Imagination; fancy. quvvat-i-mutakhaiyala . The thinking faculty; imagination.

ety: act. part. of تخلّص 'to become free, or safe,' &c., v of خلص 'to be or become pure,' &c.
def: part. adj. Freed (from); pure; picked, choice; — peculiarly named or entitled, surnamed (see taḵẖalluṣ).
more: (part. act. V. of خلص) adj. Pure; peculiarly named or entitled, surnamed.

ety: obl. dual of mutaḵẖāṣim, act. part. of تخاصم 'to contend (with one another
def: s.m. Plaintiff and defendant (in a law-suit).

ety: moṭā+Prk. क = S. क+nā, as in maṭkānā, q.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Plump; middle-sized; — pretty, good-looking.

ety: for A. مطالعة mut̤ālaʻat, inf. n. of طالع 'to inspect, contemplate; to study, peruse,' iii of طلع 'to rise; to ascend,' &c.
def: s.m. Considering, inspecting, turning the attention (to); consideration, contemplation; study; reading, perusal
more: n. m. طلع 1. Reading; perusal. 2. Study. mutālٴa k. , v. a. 1. To read; peruse. 2. To study.
(inf. III. of طلع) s. m. 1. Contemplation, consideration. 2. Reading, study, perusal. مطالعه کرنا mut̤ālaʼaﻌ karnā, To study, to read, to peruse.
subs: (-kā), To consider; to study; to read, peruse
v.t. To keep in consideration, or in view; to keep before (one); to study constantly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مطالعه (mutāla'a), from Arabic مُطَالَعَة (muṭālaʕa).

ety: for A. مطالبة mut̤ālabat, inf. n. of طالب 'to seek or den and (a thing, of a person

more: n. m. طلب 1. A demand; requisition; call. 2. A claim; due ( dain ). mutālbah khafīfah , A small Cause Court. mutālbah k. , v. a. To demand; claim.
(inf. III. of طلب) s. m. Asking for, requiring, exaction.
subs: (-kā), To demand; claim (a thing, from -se); to sue.

ety: a corr. formation, after the model of the act. part. of conj. vi of the Arabic, fr. talāsh or talāshī, q.v.
def: s.m. One who seeks or searches for, searcher; inquirer; adventurer.
more: n. m. One who seeks or searches for. mutalāshī-i-roz-gār , n. m. One in search of service.
(see تلاشي) s. m. An inquirer, searcher, adventurer.

ety: act. part. of تلاطم 'to buffet each other,' vi of لطم 'to buffet'
def: part. adj. Dashing together, agitated (as waves); buffeting one another.

ety: act. part. of تلّون 'to be coloured; to change colour' (v of لون, which is not in use

(part. act. V. of لون) adj. Changeable, variable (in colour), capricious.
subs: adj. Of capricious or changeable disposition; capricious, &c.
s.m. A capricious or fickle disposition; — adj. Capricious, &c. ( = mutalawwinuʼt̤-t̤abʻ).

ety: act. part. of تلذّذ, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Relishing, delighting (in), delighted (by), receiving pleasure (from).
more: part. act. (V. from لذة) Relishing, delighted, receiving pleasure (from any thing).

ety: act. part. of طالع; see mut̤ālaʻa
def: adj. Contemplating, looking towards; — considerate, attentive.

more: Old H. n. f. H. mūt urine, ālā wet. Stable litter.

ety: Prk. मुट्टल्लओ; S. मूर्त्त+ल+कः; see moṭā
def: adj. (f. -ī), Fat, &c. ( = moṭā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), Fat, plump, stout, corpulent; big, bulky; great, large; gross; dense, thick; coarse; inferior; capital (as a letter); material, important; rich, wealthy; — palpable, plain (e.g. moṭī bāt)
subs: To see indistinctly, to be dim-sighted
s.m. A thick reed-pen (for writing capitals with)
A wealthy or well-to-do person; a prize, a catch; — a heavy purse.

ety: for A. مطلّي mut̤allā, pass. part. of طلّي, ii of (and syn. with

(part. pass. II. of طلي Heb. and Aram. or ) adj. Gilded.

Bāzār, galī aur kūchoṅ meṅ har sāat herā pher huī, Thī chāh nazar bhar dekhne kī jis jāgah par muṭh-bheṛ huī, Ṭuk dekh līyā, dil shād kīyā, khush vaqt hue, aur chal nikle . Nazīr. def: n. m. S. मुष्टि 1. A handful. Pakṛā chuṭṭā, aur bharā muṭṭhā . Rus. Seize by the hair, a fistful's there. (Payment obtained by force). 2. A bundle. muṭṭhā bāṅdhnā, yā banānā , v. a. To make a bundle.
more: n. m. S. मथित 1. E. Butter-milk. 2. W. Tyre mixed with some water. Dahī kā maṭṭhā kar lo!
adj. 1. Slow; slack; lazy. 2. Dull ( kund , 2).

def: n. f. S. मुष्टि, Lat. manus hand. 1. The fist. Jo deṅ so muṭṭhī meṅ bāṅdh-kar le jāo, takrār na karo! Clench what you 've got in your fist and be off! dont make a row. 2. Grasp; clutch; gripe. Kyā hī muṭṭhī pakṛī hai! Prov. What a grip he has of his wrist! (Said of a wrestler). 3. A hand, equal to four fingers. 4. The fetlock joint. 5. A handful of corn formerly taken from every field and given in charity to the poor. 6. A small varnished stick given to sucking children to be used as the coral with us. muṭṭhī bhar . A handful. Kabhī ghī ghanā, kabhī muṭṭhī bhar chanā . Prov. Sometimes of butter plenty, sometimes a fistful of gram only. muṭṭhī garm k. , v. a. To bribe. muṭṭhī meṅ , 1. In one's fist. 2. Under one's power or thumb. muṭṭhī meṅ denā , v. a. 1. To place in one's hand. 2. To fill one's fist. muṭṭhiyāṅ bharnā , v. a. To shampoo. baṅdhī muṭṭhī , n. f. 1. The closed fist. 2. A bundle of sticks. 3. A compact body.

more: s. f. Fist, grasp (of the hand), clutch, hand, handful. مٿهي روٿي maṭhī roṭī, A kind of bread: see Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. xxxiv.
subs: (-kī), 'To warm the fist or hand' (of), to give a bribe (to), to bribe
In the fist (of), into the hand (of, -kī); in the clutches (of), under the power (of)
'To fill the fists'; to knead the limbs, to shampoo
s.f. The closed fist; — a bundle (of sticks, &c.); a compact body.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀺 (muṭṭhi) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुष्टि (muṣṭi), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *muštíš (“fist”). Doublet of مُشْت (muśt). Cognate with Punjabi ਮੁੱਠ (muṭṭha), Bengali মুঠা (muṭha) and Persian مشت (mošt).

def: s.f. corr. of muṭṭhī, q.v. s.f.
